2605.29796.title: SAAS: Self-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Over-Search Mitigation in Agentic Search
2605.29796.paper_summary: Agentic search enables LLMs to solve complex multi-hop questions through iterative reasoning and external search. Despite the effectiveness, these systems often suffer from a critical limitation in practice: agents fail to recognize their own knowledge boundaries, blindly triggering searches when internal knowledge suffices and failing to terminate search even when adequate evidence has been collected. The lack of self-awareness leads to severe \textbf{over-search}, incurring substantial inference latency and prohibitive computational cost. To this end, we propose SAAS, a novel RL framework designed to cultivate dynamic self-awareness that precisely regulates search behavior without compromising accuracy. SAAS introduces three key components: (i) a search boundary modeling mechanism, which identifies the search boundary under the evolving policy by contrasting search-disabled and search-enabled rollouts; (ii) a boundary-aware reward module, which translates this boundary awareness into trajectory-level penalties, suppressing unnecessary and redundant searches; and (iii) a stage-wise optimization strategy, which leverages a sequential curriculum to prioritize reasoning over search regularization, thereby avoiding reward hacking. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SAAS substantially reduces over-search, while maintaining accuracy. Our code is anonymously released at https://github.com/XMUDeepLIT/SAAS.
2605.29796.paper_summary_zh: 代理搜索使大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠通過迭代推理和外部搜索來解決複雜的多跳問題。儘管效果顯著，這些系統在實踐中經常面臨一個關鍵限制：代理未能認識到自身的知識邊界，當內部知識足夠時盲目觸發搜索，並且即使在收集到足夠證據時也未能終止搜索。缺乏自我意識導致嚴重的 \textbf{過度搜索}，造成可觀的推理延遲和高昂的計算成本。為此，我們提出了SAAS，一種新穎的強化學習框架，旨在培養動態自我意識，精確調節搜索行為而不妨礙準確性。SAAS引入了三個關鍵組件：（i）搜索邊界建模機制，通過對比禁用搜索和啟用搜索的回放，識別在不斷演變的策略下的搜索邊界；（ii）邊界感知獎勵模塊，將這種邊界意識轉化為軌跡級別的懲罰，抑制不必要和冗餘的搜索；以及（iii）階段性優化策略，利用序列課程優先考慮推理而非搜索正則化，從而避免獎勵黑客。大量實驗表明，SAAS顯著減少了過度搜索，同時保持準確性。我們的代碼已匿名發布於 https://github.com/XMUDeepLIT/SAAS。
2605.29796.author: Yunbo Tang et.al.
2605.29796.authors: Yunbo Tang, Chengyi Yang, Shiyu Liu, Zhishang Xiang, Zerui Chen, Qinggang Zhang, Jinsong Su
